items.speaker: narrator
items.instructions: Cinematic, urgent, slightly amused storyteller. Medium pace.
items.text: This is me, Salvador. By my side, running as fast as she can, is my partner, Olivia. We are being chased by the ghost of a cow.

items.speaker: narrator
items.instructions: Amused, suspenseful, inviting the listener into the story.
items.text: I know. It sounds crazy. But I promise it really happened. How did we get here? That's a good story.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: It all started at Liceo de Cabo Frio.

items.speaker: teacher
items.instructions: Older male teacher. Clear, authoritative, classroom voice.
items.text: We are going to thirty-four degrees, nine minutes, fifty-two seconds south; fifty-eight degrees, one minute, fifty-eight seconds west.

items.speaker: teacher
items.text: Does someone know where we are going?

items.speaker: teacher
items.text: Salvador?

items.speaker: salvador
items.instructions: Teen boy, intelligent, slightly proud but natural.
items.text: We're going to Conchillas, to learn more about the British influence in Uruguay.

items.speaker: teacher
items.text: Good work, Salvador!

items.speaker: teacher
items.instructions: Inviting more answers from the class.
items.text: Can anyone add anything else?

items.speaker: olivia
items.instructions: Teen girl, bright, confident, enthusiastic about history.
items.text: The town was founded by the British in eighteen eighty-seven. Conchillas was used to make the port of Buenos Aires.

items.speaker: olivia
items.text: The Walker Company extracted sand from Conchillas. David Evans arrived in nineteen ten and opened the most important shop in Conchillas, Almacén Evans.

items.speaker: teacher
items.instructions: Teacher managing a group of students.
items.text: Students, please! Let's go. Remember to find your seat on the bus.

items.speaker: salvador
items.text: How do you know so much?

items.speaker: olivia
items.text: My surname is Evans because my grandmother was from Conchillas.

items.speaker: salvador
items.text: Cool!

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Some time later, on the bus.

items.speaker: unknown_messenger
items.instructions: Neutral, slightly digital, read like a text message.
items.text: Are you the Salvador who solved the mysteries at Cape Cold?

items.speaker: salvador
items.text: True. What do you want to know?

items.speaker: unknown_messenger
items.text: What happened to your friends?

items.speaker: salvador
items.text: They are older and don't have time to solve mysteries anymore.

items.speaker: unknown_messenger
items.text: What do you think about what happened?

items.speaker: salvador
items.text: I can't stop thinking about it. I think saving the planet is very important.

items.speaker: unknown_messenger
items.text: You want to save the planet? That's big!

items.speaker: salvador
items.text: I know! But we have to do something.

items.speaker: unknown_messenger
items.text: Okay, Mister Hero, how do you save the planet?

items.speaker: salvador
items.text: I work as a volunteer for an NGO helping to protect the flora and fauna of Cape Cold.

items.speaker: unknown_messenger
items.text: That's nice. I want to help too.

items.speaker: salvador
items.text: Great! You can come with me.

items.speaker: narrator
items.instructions: Read as a sign being noticed.
items.text: Welcome to Conchillas.

items.speaker: teacher
items.instructions: Teacher addressing the class after arrival.
items.text: Welcome to Conchillas! Now, you will work in pairs.

items.speaker: olivia
items.instructions: Friendly, upbeat, inviting Salvador to partner up.
items.text: Hey, Salvador. Do you want to work with me?

items.speaker: salvador
items.text: Yeah! Why not?

items.speaker: teacher
items.text: Salvador and Olivia, you will investigate the Hotel Evans.

items.speaker: olivia
items.text: Did you know my grandmother was from Conchillas?

items.speaker: salvador
items.text: Yes, you told me in class. Have you been here before?

items.speaker: olivia
items.instructions: Reflective and fond of the place.
items.text: Yes, a few times. I really like this town. It feels different. Do you know what I mean?

items.speaker: salvador
items.text: No. Different how?

items.speaker: olivia
items.text: Like a little piece of Britain in Uruguay. Also, my grandmother always told me ghost stories about this town.

items.speaker: salvador
items.text: Oh no, really? Not ghosts again! Where do we have to go?

items.speaker: olivia
items.text: To Hotel Evans. David Evans was one of my ancestors. He was a powerful and mysterious person.

items.speaker: salvador
items.text: Of course! I get it now. Your surname is Evans.

items.speaker: olivia
items.instructions: Pleased and theatrical, leading into reveal.
items.text: That's right. Welcome to...

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Hotel Evans.
